calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-25-2015 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MARRIANNE B. SIOSON of the Law Offices of Brian L. Larsen, Tel No. (415) 398-5000 appearing for Plaintiff Sudi Scull;
Atty. MARIA M. LAMPASONA of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP, Tel No. (510) 433-2600 appearing for Defendant American Medical Response West.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. Defendant met its initial burden of establishing that the ambulance driver, Mr. Padro, was a licensed EMT responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 2-4.) Plaintiff does not present evidence to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether Mr. Padro was a licensed EMT or as to whether Mr. Padro was responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. Therefore, pursuant to Canister v. Emergency Ambulance Service (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 388, the one-year statute of limitations provided for in the Medical Injury Compensation Reform Act (MICRA) applies. (CCP sec. 340.5.) Plaintiff's injuries occurred on July 20, 2012. (Complaint, par. MV-1.) The complaint was filed on February 6, 2014, beyond the statute of limitations. Alternatively, Defendant met its initial burden of establishing that the immunity provided for in Cal. Health and Safety Code sec. 1799.106 applies because Mr. Padro was a licensed EMT responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. Defendant also introduces evidence showing that Plaintiff is not alleging gross negligence. (UMF 22-23.) "Because the vicarious liability of the employer is wholly dependent or derived from the liability of the employee, any substantive defense that is available to the employee inures to the benefit of the employer." (Lathrop v. Healthcare Partners Medical Group (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1423.) Again, Plaintiff does not present evidence to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether Mr. Padro was a licensed EMT or as to whether Mr. Padro was responding to an emergency at the time of the accident. Further, there is no evidence presented to raise a triable issue as to whether Mr. Padro acted with gross negligence. Lastly, Plaintiff's negligent entrustment claim fails as a matter of law, because there is no factual dispute that Mr. Padro was acting within the course and scope of his employment at the time of the accident, and therefore, Defendant is sued on a vicarious liability theory. If an employer admits vicarious liability for any negligent driving by its employee, the plaintiff cannot also pursue a negligent entrustment claim. (Diaz v. Carcamo (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1148, 1152.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Set For Trial On Mar-02-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
